title: trichomanes emarginatum
intro: Trichomanes emarginatum, also known as the fringeless lipfern, is a species of fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is an evergreen, epiphytic fern with fronds that are typically 10-20 cm long, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. The medicinal properties of Trichomanes emarginatum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting immune function, with herbal preparations typically involving infusions or decoctions of the plant's fronds.
